architecture.summary: The paper uses Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) trained on residual stream activations to extract interpretable features from Pythia-160M (Transformer) and Mamba-130M models. Features are compared across architectures via Maximum Pairwise Pearson Correlation (MPPC) to measure cross-architecture universality. Circuit-level analysis of induction uses path patching to trace information flow through SSM states and local convolution layers in Mamba versus attention heads in Transformers.
trained_components: Sparse Autoencoders (SAEs) for residual stream decomposition

biblio.title: Towards Universality: Studying Mechanistic Similarity Across Language Model Architectures

claims.statement: Cross-arch SAE MPPC has an average of 0.74, compared to 0.76 for model seed variant and 0.81 for SAE seed variant
More than 30% Pythia features find their matched feature in Mamba with an MPPC greater than 0.95

claims.metric: percentage of features within MPPC difference interval
claims.statement: 90% of Pythia features fall in the interval of MPPC difference to 0 under the metric comparing Cross-Arch SAE MPPC with model seed variant skyline

claims.metric: percentage of features with near-zero MPPC difference
claims.statement: More than 60% features have near-zero MPPC difference, indicating they are consistently present after architectural changes

SAE features outperform neuron baseline: neuron baseline exhibits almost zero sign of similarity between Mamba and Pythia, validating that privileged directions across architectures are not informative

If a feature in Pythia is located in the k-th layer out of 12 layers, its matched feature in Mamba is most likely to appear around layer 2k in Mamba (out of 24 layers), indicating SAE features form similar hierarchy across architectures

Mamba and Transformer implement the same induction algorithm but with structural differences: Mamba uses local convolution to merge neighboring tokens' information into SSM state, while Transformers use previous token head and induction head via attention
Mamba exhibits Off-by-One motif: information of one token is written into the SSM state in its next position, whereas interaction between tokens in Transformers does not exhibit such trend
